blocks.text: The CVT mounted on the 2WD model is a torque converter type stepless forward/stepless reverse front-wheel drive, with the transmission located on the left side of the engine. The CVT is electronically controlled by a PGM-FI ECU that controls gear shifting and lock-up.

items.text: The torque converter is connected to the drive plate, which is directly connected to the engine crankshaft. The torque converter also has a starter gear and flywheel mass. The torque converter has an integrated lock-up clutch that transfers engine power directly to the input shaft during lock-up.

The input shaft engaging the torque converter is an integral part of the forward clutch comp. When the forward clutch comp. is engaged, the forward drive gear assembled to the input shaft drives the drive pulley comp. The forward clutch comp. is a non-disassembled component.
The drive pulley Comp. has a reverse clutch Comp. and reverse driven gear that constitute the forward/reverse changeover mechanism.
The final drive gear is splined to the driven pulley comp.

Drive Pulley Comp. and Driven Pulley Comp. are assembled in the transmission case, and both pulleys are connected by a metal belt. Drive Pulley Comp. and Driven Pulley Comp. are non-disassembled parts.

The oil pump is a trochoidal pump. The oil pump drive sprocket assembled in the transmission case via bearings is connected to the oil pump driven sprocket on the oil pump side by an oil pump drive chain. The oil pump is constantly driven by a torque converter connected to the drive plate directly connected to the engine crankshaft during engine rotation.
The differential assy. is built into the transmission and is driven by the final drive gear on the final drive shaft via the final driven gear.
The valve body assembly, which incorporates the hydraulic control valves, etc., and the oil pump are mounted on the transmission case.
Electronic control components such as shift position sensors, oil pressure sensors, and speed sensors are mounted on the outside of the transmission.
Transmission fluid warmer is equipped to accelerate transmission fluid temperature rise during cold operation.
A CVT accumulator is provided to maintain transmission hydraulic pressure when the idling stop system is activated and engine/transmission rotation is stopped.

blocks.text: The CVT mounted on the 4WD model is a torque converter type stepless forward and stepless reverse all-wheel drive, with the transmission located on the left side of the engine. The CVT is electronically controlled by a PGM-FI ECU that controls gear shifting and lock-up.

items.text: The torque converter is connected to the drive plate, which is directly connected to the engine crankshaft. The torque converter also has a starter gear and flywheel mass. The torque converter has an integrated lock-up clutch that transfers engine power directly to the input shaft during lock-up.

The input shaft engaging the torque converter is an integral part of the forward clutch comp. When the forward clutch comp. is engaged, the forward drive gear assembled to the input shaft drives the drive pulley comp. The forward clutch Comp. is a non-disassembled component.
The drive pulley Comp. has a reverse clutch Comp. and reverse driven gear that constitute the forward/reverse changeover mechanism.
The final drive gear is splined to the driven pulley comp.

Drive Pulley Comp. and Driven Pulley Comp. are assembled in the transmission case, and both pulleys are connected by a metal belt. Drive Pulley Comp. and Driven Pulley Comp. are non-disassembled parts.

The oil pump is a trochoidal pump. The oil pump drive sprocket assembled in the transmission case via bearings is connected to the oil pump driven sprocket on the oil pump side by an oil pump drive chain. The oil pump is constantly driven by a torque converter connected to the drive plate directly connected to the engine crankshaft during engine rotation.
The differential assy. is built into the transmission and is driven by the final drive gear on the final drive shaft via the final driven gear.

The transfer that distributes power to the rear wheels is a cassette type and is mounted on the transmission case. The transfer driven gear meshes with the differential's final driven gear to transmit power to the rear wheels via the transfer.
The valve body assembly, which incorporates the hydraulic control valves, etc., and the oil pump are mounted on the transmission case.
Electronic control components such as shift position sensors, oil pressure sensors, and speed sensors are mounted on the outside of the transmission.
Transmission fluid warmer is equipped to accelerate transmission fluid temperature rise during cold operation.
A CVT accumulator is provided to maintain transmission hydraulic pressure when the idling stop system is activated and engine/transmission rotation is stopped.

blocks.text: The input shaft is integral with the forward clutch comp. and when the forward clutch is engaged, the forward drive gear assembled to the input shaft drives the drive pulley comp. When the D, S, or L position (vehicles not equipped with paddle shift switch) is selected, the manual valve moves to form an oil path to the forward clutch comp. and the forward clutch is connected.

When the R position is selected, the manual valve moves to form the oil path to the reverse clutch and the reverse clutch is connected. When the reverse clutch is engaged, the reverse drive gear and reverse idle gear cause the drive pulley comp. to rotate in the reverse direction from the forward direction.
The metal belt connecting the drive pulley Comp. and the driven pulley Comp. consists of two bundles of rings and about 400 elements.

The V-face of the element contacts the pulley faces of the drive pulley comp. and driven pulley comp. and is pressed down strongly from both sides to transfer engine power from the drive pulley comp. to the driven pulley comp.
